q: Arrhenius acids are any substances that dissociate to produce hydrogen ions ($H^{+}$) when they dissolve in water.
Arrhenius bases are any substances that dissociate to produce hydroxide ions ($OH^{-}$) when they dissolve in non-aqueous solvents.
Strong acids such as hydrochloric acid completely ionise in water.
Weak acids such as ethanoic acid completely ionise in water.
Arrhenius theory is unable to account for the acidic properties of $CO_{2}$, $SO_{2}$, and $SO_{3}$, which do not have hydrogen.
According to the Brønsted-Lowry theory, acids are proton acceptors and bases are proton donors.
The conjugate base of a Brønsted-Lowry acid is the species that remains when one proton has been removed from the acid.
A strong acid has a strong conjugate base and a strong base has a strong conjugate acid.
Lewis bases are species that can donate an electron pair, while acids are species that can accept an electron pair.
The Lewis concept of acids and bases defines acids exclusively as species that can donate a proton.
The reaction of ammonia with boron trifluoride is classified as an acid-base reaction according to the Lewis theory.
The $NH_{3}$ molecule behaves as a Lewis acid because it possesses a lone pair of electrons shared with $BF_{3}$.
The Brønsted-Lowry definition of acids and bases applies to all solvents, not only to aqueous solutions.
Brønsted-Lowry theory explains the reaction between acidic oxides and basic oxides because there is significant proton transfer.
A conjugate acid results from the addition of a proton to a Brønsted-Lowry base.
According to the text, a strong base has a weak conjugate acid and vice versa.
The Lewis concept is more general than any other definition which defines only the involvement of hydrogen ions or protons.
Lewis theory defines acids as substances that dissociate to produce hydrogen ions ($H^{+}$) in water.
Every Brønsted-Lowry acid and base has a corresponding conjugate base and conjugate acid.
The $BF_{3}$ molecule, which accepts an electron pair, behaves as a Lewis base.
